evidence_summary: No literature found linking APRIL (TNFSF13) to induction of Decorin in immune cells or any context. APRIL drives B cell/plasma cell biology and TACI/BCMA signaling; Decorin is an ECM proteoglycan regulated by TGF-beta, not APRIL.
search_queries_used: APRIL TNFSF13 induces Decorin expression immune cells
APRIL decorin proteoglycan ECM extracellular matrix

evidence_summary: Type I interferons (including IFN-omega, signaling through the same IFNAR receptor as IFN-alpha/beta) upregulate IL-15 expression in vitro and in vivo, priming CD8 T and NK cells.
citations: Interferon-alpha/beta upregulate IL-15 expression in vitro and in vivo - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11030826/
IFN-alpha primes T- and NK-cells for IL-15-mediated signaling and cytotoxicity - https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0161589011006705
Soluble IL-15 Complexes Generated by Type I IFN Pathways - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4354909/
search_queries_used: IFN-omega induces IL-15 CD8 T cells
type I interferon induces IL-15 expression
IFN-omega type I interferon antiviral IL-15 induction

evidence_summary: Direction is reversed in literature: IL-36 signaling (via IL-36R/MyD88/NF-kB) drives Th9 differentiation and IL-9 production in CD4+ T cells, not the other way around.
citations: IL-36gamma signaling controls iTreg-Th9 balance - https://www.nature.com/articles/mi201721
IL-36 cytokines imprint a colitogenic phenotype on CD4+ T helper cells - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9038530/
search_queries_used: IL-9 IL-36 alpha CD4 T cell axis

evidence_summary: MAIT cells highly express IL17RE (receptor for IL-17C) and IL-17C is epithelium-derived (induced by IL-1beta/TNF/TLR2/TLR5), not by IL-9. No evidence IL-9 induces IL-17C in MAIT cells or elsewhere.
citations: MAIT cells and the microbiome (IL-17RE expression) - https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2023.1127588/full
IL-17C: Unique Epithelial Cytokine - https://www.jidonline.org/article/S0022-202X(18)30223-9/fulltext

evidence_summary: No published link found between APRIL and EPO induction in monocytes. APRIL is produced by monocytes (not the reverse), and EPO is hypoxia-regulated by HIF in kidney/liver; the two are studied independently.

Tumor-derived IL-35 promotes VEGF secretion and angiogenesis via STAT3 activation in myeloid cells (CD11b+Gr1+, neutrophils). Context-dependent: pro-angiogenic in tumors, anti-VEGF in rheumatoid arthritis.
citations: Tumor-derived IL-35 promotes tumor growth by enhancing myeloid cell accumulation and angiogenesis - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3578001/
IL-35 enhances angiogenic effects of small extracellular vesicles in breast cancer - https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35037402/
Mathematical modeling of IL-35 promoting tumor growth and angiogenesis - https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0110126

evidence_summary: Direction is reversed: IL-27 is a robust SUPPRESSOR of GM-CSF in T cells (STAT1-dependent), well documented in EAE and CNS inflammation. Co-regulated but A→B is anti-cascade.
citations: Suppression of GM-CSF expression in murine and human T cells by IL-27 - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3424384/
IL-27, but not IL-35, inhibits neuroinflammation through modulating GM-CSF expression - https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-017-16702-w
search_queries_used: IL-27 induces GM-CSF production T cells

evidence_summary: Direction is reversed: IL-27 is a well-documented SUPPRESSOR of Th9 differentiation and IL-9 production in naive CD4+ T cells, partially via STAT1/T-bet.
citations: IL-27 inhibits Th9 differentiation while inducing Tr1 cells - https://www.researchgate.net/figure/L-27-inhibits-Th9-differentiation-while-inducing-Tr1-cells-Real-time-PCR-and-flow_fig7_232813959
Updated advancement of bifunctional IL-27 in inflammatory autoimmune diseases - https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2024.1366377/full

evidence_summary: Indirect link: IL-27 induces Th1 differentiation via STAT1/T-bet, and Th1 cells produce lymphotoxin (alongside IFN-gamma). No direct study of IL-27 inducing LT-alpha2-beta1 specifically in CD8 cells.
citations: Interleukin-27 in T Cell Immunity - https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4346869/
IL-27 elicits a cytotoxic CD8+ T cell program - https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-024-08510-w
search_queries_used: IL-27 lymphotoxin LT alpha beta induction

evidence_summary: IL-6 promotes Th2 polarization (inducing IL-4 secretion and inhibiting Th1), and Th2 cells are the canonical IL-13 source. No direct mechanistic study of IL-6 inducing IL-13 transcription in naive CD4 T cells.
citations: Interleukin 6: at the interface of human health and disease - https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2023.1255533/full
search_queries_used: IL-6 induces IL-13 expression T cells

evidence_summary: VEGF enhances Th2 sensitization and inflammation in the lung, with a documented positive feedback loop with IL-13. VEGF induces IL-13-dependent asthma-like phenotype in transgenic mice.
citations: VEGF induces remodeling and enhances TH2-mediated sensitization - https://www.nature.com/articles/nm1105
VEGF controls lung Th2 inflammation via miR-1-Mpl-P-selectin axis - https://rupress.org/jem/article/210/10/1993/41405/VEGF-controls-lung-Th2-inflammation-via-the-miR-1
Studies of VEGF in asthma and COPD - https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22052929/
search_queries_used: VEGF Th2 induces IL-13 lung asthma

evidence_summary: IL-36 (including IL-36alpha) activates Th17 cells and induces IL-17A expression, forming a positive feedback loop with IL-17A in psoriasis. Direct evidence in T cells via Th17 differentiation.
citations: Biology of IL-36 Signaling and Its Role in Systemic Inflammatory Diseases - https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2019.02532/full
Inter-Regulation of Th17 Cytokines and IL-36 Cytokines - https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0022202X15351137
IL-17/IL-36 axis in skin inflammation - https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0222969

evidence_summary: No published link between IL-19 (IL-10 family) and APRIL induction in any immune cell type, including MAIT cells. IL-19 signals through IL-20R complexes/STAT3; APRIL is induced by TLR9, IFN-alpha, and B cell activation independently.
search_queries_used: IL-19 induces APRIL TNFSF13 B cells
IL-19 APRIL plasma cells IL-10 family
IL-19 induces B cell APRIL plasma differentiation

evidence_summary: GM-CSF induces TL1A (Tnfsf15) expression in macrophages, demonstrated in intestinal inflammation models. ILC3 effects on macrophage TL1A were GM-CSF-dependent.
citations: GM-CSF Calibrates Macrophage Defense and Wound Healing Programs - https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S221112472030838X
TL1A/TNFSF15 directly induces proinflammatory cytokines from CD3+CD161+ T cells - https://www.mucosalimmunology.org/article/S1933-0219(22)01244-2/fulltext

evidence_summary: No literature found linking APRIL to IL-36Ra induction in NKT or any other immune cells. The two molecules operate in distinct pathways (APRIL: TACI/BCMA on B cells; IL-36Ra: IL-36R competitive antagonism in epithelia/B cells).
search_queries_used: APRIL IL-36 receptor antagonist B cell production

evidence_summary: GITRL is induced in keratinocytes by Th2 cytokines/TNF-alpha and on APCs by various stimuli, but no published evidence of VEGF inducing GITRL in NKT cells or any cell type.

Direction is reversed: GITRL/GITR co-stimulation drives Th9 differentiation and IL-9 production from CD4 T cells (key axis in anti-tumor immunity), not the other way around. IL-9 and GITRL co-occur in the Th9 axis.
citations: GITR drives TH9-mediated antitumor immunity - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4910720/
GITR subverts Foxp3+ Tregs to boost Th9 immunity - https://www.nature.com/articles/ncomms9266
search_queries_used: IL-9 mast cells GITR GITRL costimulation

evidence_summary: No published link between cardiotrophin-1 (IL-6 family/gp130) and APRIL induction. CT-1 induces IL-6, TNF-alpha, and VEGF via JAK2/STAT3, but no evidence for APRIL induction in MAIT or other cells.
Direction is reversed: cardiotrophin-1 induces VEGF expression in cardiac myocytes (via STAT3), well documented. No evidence of VEGF inducing CT-1.
citations: Cardiotrophin-1 induces VEGF expression in cardiac myocytes - https://www.pnas.org/doi/pdf/10.1073/pnas.92.4.1142
VEGF-A in Cardiomyocytes and Heart Diseases - https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7432634/
search_queries_used: VEGF cardiotrophin CT-1 IL-6 family induction

evidence_summary: IL-9 and IL-15 share the gamma chain receptor but no evidence IL-9 induces IL-15 production. IL-15 (not IL-9) regulates NKT homeostasis, terminal maturation, and stage 3 differentiation.
